metadata.title: retinal_sim validation audit report
warnings: Visual-inspection checks remain labeled as visual/manual evidence, not strict quantitative proof.
Several validation checks use proof-of-concept simplifications documented in SCRATCHPAD.md.

architecture_coverage.architecture_ref: Architecture §11e Retinal image scaling across species

architecture_coverage.note: Counts stimulated receptors inside the projected object footprint.
Uses a fixed deterministic near-metamer regression dataset under D65.
Uses the same D65 observer model as the basis construction for reprojection.
Uses deterministic RGB probes to sanity-check cone-catch ordering under the D65-based reconstruction model.
Measures empirical sinusoidal contrast transfer against the implemented PSF MTF.

architecture_coverage.architecture_ref: Architecture §2a Pupil throughput and aperture geometry
architecture_coverage.note: Report-layer pupil-area diagnostic normalised to the human reference pupil.

architecture_coverage.architecture_ref: Architecture §2b Slit pupil anisotropic blur diagnostics
architecture_coverage.note: Report-layer elliptical Gaussian point-response diagnostic for the cat slit pupil.
Checks signed LCA offsets and minimum blur near the 555 nm reference focus.
Samples species media transmission tables and visualizes delivered spectra.
Uses fixed confusion/control stimulus matrices with median-based comparisons.

architecture_coverage.architecture_ref: Architecture §11c Cone density to sampling limit
architecture_coverage.note: Converts local cone density to cycles/degree using focal-length magnification.
Compares measured 1 mm² counts against density-model expectations.
Quantifies center-vs-periphery contrast loss on a checkerboard stimulus.

results.inputs_summary: Three analytic scene width/distance cases using the human optical model.
assumptions: Exact 2*arctan geometry is the source of truth.
Human optical focal length is used only to build SceneDescription.
results.method: Compare computed angular width against the closed-form geometric equation.
results.pass_criterion: Absolute angular error < 0.01 degrees for every case.
limitations: Checks representative cases, not exhaustive parameter sweeps.
artifacts: Bar chart: expected vs computed angular width

results.architecture_ref: Architecture §11e Retinal image scaling across species
results.inputs_summary: One shared physical scene evaluated for human, dog, and cat species configs.
assumptions: Retinal image width should scale with focal length when angular size is fixed.
results.pass_criterion: Species ratios agree within 1% relative error.
limitations: Only validates proportionality, not downstream sampling behavior.

results.architecture_ref: Architecture §0 Accommodation demand and residual defocus
results.inputs_summary: Near-view scene at 0.25 m for human, dog, and cat.
assumptions: Accommodation is a hard cutoff.
Max accommodation values are fixed PoC constants.
results.method: Compare computed residual defocus against max(0, demand - max_accommodation).
results.pass_criterion: Residual defocus matches expected value within 0.1 diopters.
limitations: No lead/lag accommodation response model is applied.
artifacts: Expected vs computed residual defocus bars

results.inputs_summary: Constant-size grayscale object that remains smaller than the 2° patch across all tested distances.
assumptions: Stimulated receptors are those whose mapped positions fall inside the projected object footprint.
results.method: Measure stimulated receptor counts at multiple viewing distances and compare against ideal inverse-square scaling.
results.pass_criterion: Far/near stimulated-receptor scaling stays within 40% relative error of ideal 1/d^2.
limitations: Density gradients and stochastic mosaic sampling still introduce some deviation from exact inverse-square behaviour.
artifacts: Distance vs stimulated receptor count plot
Log-log ideal vs measured scaling plot

results.inputs_summary: Fixed near-metamer RGB pair dataset integrated against human receptor sensitivities under D65.
assumptions: Dataset is repo-local and deterministic to keep the audit reproducible.
results.method: Upsample each RGB pair, integrate against human sensitivities, and bound the maximum cone-catch relative difference.
results.pass_criterion: Maximum cone-catch relative difference < 3% for every dataset pair.
limitations: Dataset is compact rather than exhaustive; it is intended as a deterministic regression panel.

results.inputs_summary: Eight canonical sRGB colors passed through spectral upsampling and D65-based reprojection.
assumptions: The same D65-referenced observer model used to build the basis spectra is used for reprojection.
results.method: Project upsampled spectra back to sRGB and compute per-colour RMSE.
results.pass_criterion: RMSE < 2 8-bit counts for all tested colours.
limitations: This validates the D65 round-trip path, not arbitrary illuminant changes.

results.inputs_summary: Deterministic RGB probe panel spanning violet-to-red anchors plus mixed colors, integrated with D65-weighted species sensitivities.
assumptions: The panel is RGB-defined and deterministic; it is a regression sanity check rather than a physical monochromator dataset.
results.method: Upsample each probe color, compute D65-weighted cone catches for each species, and verify expected dominance/order and blue-vs-warm separations.
results.pass_criterion: Anchor colors preserve expected cone-type dominance and dog/cat blue-vs-yellow/red separations exceed the fixed margin.
limitations: Because the probes originate from sRGB values, this validates response ordering under the current D65-based reconstruction model rather than true narrowband physiology.

results.expected: Measured sinusoidal contrast transfer matches predicted MTF within 0.081
results.details:
5 cpd: measured=0.9897, predicted=0.9897, |Δ|=0.0000 ✓
10 cpd: measured=0.9597, predicted=0.9597, |Δ|=0.0000 ✓
20 cpd: measured=0.8495, predicted=0.8495, |Δ|=0.0000 ✓
30 cpd: measured=0.7787, predicted=0.6983, |Δ|=0.0804 ✓
results.inputs_summary: Human 550 nm PSF tested with sinusoidal gratings spanning low-to-mid spatial frequencies.
assumptions: The implemented PSF is a diffraction-derived Gaussian approximation.
results.method: Measure empirical contrast transfer on sinusoidal gratings and compare it with the PSF-predicted MTF.
results.pass_criterion: Measured modulation stays within 0.08 of the predicted MTF at all tested spatial frequencies.
limitations: Validation targets the implemented diffraction-derived PSF model, not a full Airy-pattern optical stack.

human: max |sum-1| = 2.22e-16 ✓

cat: max |sum-1| = 2.22e-16 ✓

cat: max |sum-1| = 2.22e-16 ✓
results.inputs_summary: Gaussian PSF kernels across the canonical wavelength grid for all three species.
assumptions: Kernel normalization in float64 is the source of truth for this check.
results.method: Sum each PSF kernel and measure max absolute deviation from 1.0.
limitations: Only validates discrete kernel normalization, not broader optical energy accounting.
artifacts: Log-scale wavelength vs normalization error plot

results.expected: Species pupil areas differ and scale throughput relative to the human reference pupil
results.actual: total_energy={human=1024.0000, dog=1820.4448, cat=1592.8888}; ratios_vs_human={human=1.000, dog=1.778, cat=1.556}; max_ratio_error=0.0000

dog: pupil_shape=circular, pupil_area_mm2=12.5664, total_retinal_energy=1820.4448, throughput_vs_human=1.778, expected_ratio=1.778
cat: pupil_shape=slit, pupil_area_mm2=10.9956, total_retinal_energy=1592.8888, throughput_vs_human=1.556, expected_ratio=1.556
Throughput is normalized to the human pupil area reference; the optical stage measurement uses a unit spectral field and a fixed scene scale.
results.architecture_ref: Architecture §2a Pupil throughput and aperture geometry
results.inputs_summary: Human, dog, and cat species configs with circular/slit pupil geometries.
assumptions: Pupil area is the relevant throughput proxy for the validation report.
Cat slit height is read from the species optical config when present.
results.method: Compute pupil area for each species, normalize to the human reference pupil, and compare the resulting throughput ratios.
results.pass_criterion: Species pupil areas differ and human-normalized throughput increases from human to dog/cat.
limitations: This is a report-layer optical-throughput diagnostic rather than an end-to-end irradiance measurement.

results.expected: Cat slit pupil produces sigma_x > sigma_y and a wider horizontal cross-section
results.actual: sigma_x_px=2.149, sigma_y_px=1.011, FWHM_x_px=5, FWHM_y_px=3; psf_sigma_mm_x=0.0021, psf_sigma_mm_y=0.0010

Point-response diagnostic uses the current optical-stage slit kernel and metadata.
results.architecture_ref: Architecture §2b Slit pupil anisotropic blur diagnostics
results.inputs_summary: Cat slit pupil evaluated with a point-response diagnostic at 550 nm.
assumptions: The diagnostic uses an elliptical Gaussian approximation for the slit pupil.
Horizontal blur should exceed vertical blur for the vertical slit approximation.
results.method: Generate a point-spread diagnostic, measure axis-specific sigma/FWHM, and require horizontal spread to exceed vertical spread.
results.pass_criterion: Cat diagnostic reports sigma_x > sigma_y and FWHM_x > FWHM_y while remaining normalized.
limitations: This report-layer approximation anticipates the axis-aware optical stage rather than replacing it.

results.expected: Short and long wavelengths carry opposite-signed LCA offsets and the smallest PSF width occurs near 555 nm
results.inputs_summary: Canonical wavelength grid evaluated for all three species using the current Gaussian-plus-LCA optical model.
assumptions: R2 models LCA as a signed wavelength-dependent defocus offset around a 555 nm reference focus.
The smallest blur should occur near the reference wavelength when scene defocus is zero.
results.method: Sample per-wavelength LCA offsets and PSF widths from the optical-stage metadata and verify sign/order behavior.
results.pass_criterion: Offsets are positive in the short wavelengths, negative in the long wavelengths, and minimum PSF width occurs near 555 nm for each species.
limitations: This validates the implemented R2 operational model, not a full empirical aberration fit.

results.inputs_summary: Species ocular-media transmission tables sampled on the canonical wavelength grid and applied to a unit spectrum.
assumptions: R2 uses fixed species reference transmission curves rather than age- or state-dependent media variants.
Short-wavelength attenuation should exceed long-wavelength attenuation for each species.
results.method: Sample the configured transmission curves, compare short-vs-long wavelength attenuation, and visualize the delivered spectra.
results.pass_criterion: Each species transmits less blue than red and the sampled transmission curves differ across species.
limitations: Does not model scatter or age-dependent lens brunescence in R2.

results.expected: human < dog ≈ cat; within published ranges
results.details:
human: predicted 2.0 arcmin (expected 1.0–5.0) ✓
dog: predicted 8.0 arcmin (expected 3.0–20.0) ✓
cat: predicted 8.0 arcmin (expected 3.0–20.0) ✓
Ordering human ≤ dog ≤ cat: human=2.0, dog=8.0, cat=8.0 ✓
results.inputs_summary: Species-specific acuity prediction using Snellen E orientation discriminability.
assumptions: Published acuity ranges are broad PoC acceptance bands.
Letter-region masking and Pearson correlation distance are required.
results.method: Predict minimum resolvable angular size for each species and compare with expected ranges and ordering.
results.pass_criterion: Each species falls within its target range and human acuity is best.
limitations: Cat predictions can be seed-sensitive near threshold.

results.inputs_summary: Fixed confusion-pair panels for dog and cat plus a shared control-pair panel.
assumptions: Stimulus scaling is applied to keep Naka-Rushton responses out of saturation.
results.method: Compute species-wise discriminability distributions across deterministic confusion/control stimulus matrices and compare confusion medians against controls.
results.pass_criterion: Each species-specific confusion median is suppressed relative to its controls, and human exceeds cat on the cat confusion set.
limitations: Compact panel intended for deterministic regression rather than exhaustive colour-space coverage.

results.architecture_ref: Architecture §11c Cone density to sampling limit
results.inputs_summary: Area-centralis cone densities converted to spacing and cycles/degree for all species.
assumptions: Hexagonal spacing approximation is used to map cone density to Nyquist limit.
results.method: Convert local cone density to spacing, then convert spacing to cycles/degree using focal-length-based retinal magnification.
results.pass_criterion: Each species falls inside its target cycles/degree band.
limitations: Uses density-model predictions at the patch centre rather than a full eccentricity sweep.

cat: measured=191710, expected=192048, count_err=0.002, cone_ratio_err=0.010 ✓
results.inputs_summary: Generated mosaics compared against density-model expectations over the simulated square patch.
assumptions: Species density functions are the literature-backed PoC source of truth for expected counts.
results.method: Compare measured receptor counts and cone/rod composition against integrated density-model expectations over the simulated patch.
results.pass_criterion: Total count error < 25% and cone/rod composition error < 15% for every species.
limitations: Current model still uses simplified analytic density functions rather than digitised histology maps.

results.expected: Human sees R/G difference; dog does not
results.details:
human: L/R diff = 0.2634
dog: L/R diff = 0.1602
cat: L/R diff = 0.1961
Human > Dog*1.2: ✓
results.inputs_summary: Red-green split stimulus simulated for human, dog, and cat.
assumptions: Left/right response norm is used as the summary metric.
results.method: Measure left/right cone-response difference after full-pipeline simulation.
results.pass_criterion: Human left/right difference exceeds dog by at least 20%.
limitations: This is a compact proxy for a richer end-to-end color-deficit comparison.

results.inputs_summary: Checkerboard stimulus simulated for human, dog, and cat.
assumptions: Checkerboard bright/dark contrast is used as the resolvability proxy.
results.method: Compare center vs peripheral bright/dark response contrast on a fine checkerboard and compare human center contrast against dichromat center contrast.
results.pass_criterion: Human center contrast exceeds human periphery and exceeds dog/cat center contrast.
limitations: Quantifies contrast retention on a fine checkerboard but does not yet fit a full eccentricity-dependent MTF curve.
